hypothesis: While in Atlanta, she received critical acclaim and was known for innovative urban education. In 2009, she was honored with the National Superintendent of the Year award. At the time of the award, Atlanta schools had seen massive improvements in test scores. Shortly thereafter, The Atlanta Journal-Constitution published a report pointing out problems with the test results. The report showed that test scores had risen rapidly and alleged that the school district had discovered internal problems but failed to act on the findings. Evidence subsequently obtained indicated that test answer sheets had been tampered with. Hall and 34 other education officials were indicted in 2013.

Controversy erupted over the spending on relief and reconstruction in relation to Hurricane Katrina, which some fiscal conservatives humorously labeled as "Bush's New Orleans Deal." Liberal criticism of the reconstruction efforts has focused on the awarding of reconstruction contracts to alleged Washington insiders.
